description_long: This sculpture depicts a humanoid figure with elongated, tentacle-like appendages extending from its back and sides, merging with its arms and lower body. The figure stands on a rectangular plinth adorned with patches of green moss and an inscription featuring stylized, possibly fictional script. The surface of the sculpture is predominantly a weathered gray stone, with subtle shading and textural details that emphasize the skeletal chest, sunken face, and flowing appendages. The back view reveals a mass of intertwined tentacles, while the front highlights the figure's downcast head and emaciated torso. The overall aesthetic is eerie and otherworldly, evoking themes of cosmic horror or mythological beings. The moss and inscription add an aged, mysterious quality to the piece.
description: A weathered gray stone sculpture of a humanoid figure with tentacle-like appendages, standing on a mossy plinth with an inscribed base. The figure has a skeletal chest, downcast head, and an eerie, otherworldly presence.
description_view_0: Side view showing the elongated tentacles flowing down the figure's back and side, with moss patches visible on the plinth.
description_view_1: Frontal view highlighting the skeletal chest, downcast head, and tentacles merging with the arms; inscription and moss are visible on the base.
description_view_2: Opposite side view emphasizing the curvature and mass of the tentacles, with more moss on the plinth.
description_view_3: Rear view displaying the full cluster of tentacles extending from the back, with moss growing between them and on the base.
attribute_location_context: No specific setting is visible; the sculpture appears isolated against a plain background.
